summary: Finalization pass completed for the public-safe web-capture example package. The package contains the required five files, uses English public-repository prose, cites primary/official sources, documents the no-live-capture limitation, and passed the saved verification helper with no missing files, privacy issues, source gaps, or coverage gaps.
next_angle: Lifecycle completion; no further research is needed unless a reviewer requests a synthetic capture fixture.

sources.title: RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol
sources.note: Primary source for robots.txt behavior and the distinction between crawler coordination and authorization.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.note: Primary source for safe HTTP methods, redirects, Location handling, and user-agent guidance.

sources.title: Python urllib.parse documentation
sources.note: Official source for URL parsing behavior and security-sensitive validation cautions.

sources.title: OWASP Server-Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Primary defensive guidance for URL-controlled server-side fetches, allowlists, parser issues, and network containment.

sources.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Primary defensive guidance for treating external content as untrusted input and screening model-driven actions.

sources.title: CommonMark Spec 0.31.2
sources.note: Primary Markdown syntax source, including raw HTML constructs relevant to render-safety boundaries.

sources.title: Microsoft MarkItDown repository
sources.note: Illustrative converter reference only; the package remains converter-neutral.

findings.text: The final package directly answers the goal: it explains web-to-Markdown capture risks, gives a staged agent workflow, and names the artifacts evaluators should expect.

The verification helper confirmed all required files are present, all seven expected source URLs appear in sources.md, and safety coverage terms for URL policy, fetch bounds, Markdown handling, prompt injection, workflow stages, artifacts, and provider neutrality are present.
The verification helper found no private workspace paths, owner identifiers, messaging identifiers, local-only tool names, secret-like literals, or Cyrillic text in the public-facing package files.
The package intentionally omits a live capture transcript to avoid redistributing unknown page content; this is documented as a limitation and does not block the requested example package.

analysis_artifacts.note: Re-runnable package validation helper used for the finalization check.
Saved verification output showing required files, source coverage, safety-term coverage, and privacy scan results.
Review-ready five-file public example package candidate.

adequacy.goal_alignment: The evidence and generated package answer the user's goal: a public-safe repository example for safe web-to-Markdown capture, including risks, workflow, and useful review artifacts.
adequacy.coverage_summary: Covered the requested five-file package, English public-facing writing, provider/converter neutrality, privacy constraints, primary/official source backing, safety risks, workflow stages, artifact checklist, and the no-live-capture limitation.

covered_requirements.evidence: Package candidate contains README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md.
Verification helper passed privacy/local-term scan and confirmed provider-neutral and converter-neutral coverage terms.
final-report.md and research-plan.md cover permission/politeness, request safety, URL/network containment, conversion boundaries, prompt injection, and intake-through-verify stages.
sources.md includes RFC 9309, RFC 9110, Python documentation, OWASP SSRF, OWASP LLM prompt-injection guidance, CommonMark, and an official illustrative converter reference.
Final verification confirms adequacy; omission of live capture transcript is documented as an intentional limitation, not a blocker.

evidence_risks.risk: No live capture transcript is included, so reviewers cannot inspect a concrete captured page in this package.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Complete lifecycle and await human review or a request for a synthetic capture fixture.

validation_evidence.reason: The candidate package provides the requested example workflow, risk framing, artifact checklist, and source-backed final report.
Automated scan found no private paths, messaging identifiers, local-only tool names, secret-like literals, or non-English/Cyrillic public text.
All five requested files exist in the package candidate.
The package cites seven official or primary references across HTTP, robots.txt, URL parsing, SSRF, prompt injection, Markdown syntax, and converter context.
The only notable limitation, lack of a live capture transcript, is documented and compatible with the public-safe package requirement.
No contradictions were found between the package files, verification report, and source list.

final_report_markdown: Review-ready package candidate: workspace/outputs/web-capture-evaluation. It contains README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md for a public repository example on safe web-to-Markdown capture. Verification passed with no missing required files, no privacy/local identifier findings, complete expected source coverage, and complete safety/workflow/artifact coverage terms.

finalization.inferred_user_need: A human-reviewable, public-safe repository example package that can be copied into examples/web-capture-evaluation/ to demonstrate safe web-to-Markdown capture in agent research workflows.
finalization.intended_recipient: Repository maintainer or evaluator reviewing the Research Mode example package before public inclusion.

internal_artifacts.note: Earlier scaffold used to shape the package.
Workflow mapping used to check stage coverage.
Machine-readable validation results from the finalization check.

candidate_artifacts.note: Review-ready five-file public repository example package.

nonblocking_defects.defect: No synthetic capture fixture is included.
nonblocking_defects.impact: Reviewers can evaluate the workflow and acceptance checks, but not a concrete capture transcript; the limitation is documented.

revisions.change: Performed final human-read inspection of the five package files and re-ran the verification helper.

validation_evidence.evidence: README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md are present.
verify_package.py found no private paths, owner names, messaging identifiers, local-only tool names, secret-like literals, or Cyrillic text.
sources.md contains all seven expected official/primary source URLs.
verify_package.py confirmed coverage terms for risk categories, workflow stages, expected artifacts, and provider/converter neutrality.
The package reads as a public repository example rather than an internal workspace dump, and the no-live-capture limitation is explicit.
